inlineContent.text: A 2-element structure that can be used to represent 2D coordinates or any other pair of numeric values.

It uses floating-point coordinates. By default, these floating-point values use 32-bit precision, unlike float which is always 64-bit. If double precision is needed, compile the engine with the option
In a boolean context, a Vector2 will evaluate to
. Otherwise, a Vector2 will always evaluate to

. This can be used to compare the angle between two vectors. For example, this can be used to determine whether an enemy is facing the player.

Returns a new vector with all components rounded to the nearest integer, with halfway cases rounded away from zero.

Returns the negative value of the Vector2. This is the same as writing Vector2(-v.x, -v.y). This operation flips the direction of the vector while keeping the same magnitude. With floats, the number zero can be either positive or negative.
Returns a new vector “bounced off” from a plane defined by the given normal.
Returns the angle between the line connecting the two points and the X axis, in radians.

back out of a Variant.
